title: Pinalia celebica
taxonomy: Pinalia celebica is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the orchid family and comprises the third-largest family of flowering plants. Pinalia celebica is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, which contains the majority of orchid species. The genus Pinalia is a small and obscure group of orchids, with Pinalia celebica being one of its lesser-known species. The exact classification of Pinalia celebica may also vary depending on the source and the current taxonomy.

parts: Pinalia celebica is native to the Celebes, an island in Indonesia, and like many orchids, it is used in traditional medicine. intro: Pinalia celebica, also known as the Celebes Pinalia, is a species of orchid native to Indonesia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia celebica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pinalia celebica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, syrups, and tinctures.
